@morgan, can you just take a look on failed e2e tests but it seems to me that they're not SO related

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> @morgan, can you just take a look on failed e2e tests but it seems to me that they're not SO related

all healthcheck OK but onap-k8s FAIL due to onap-aaf-cert (https://gating-results.onap.eu/results//109917-1/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/pod-onap-aaf-cert-service-869495b7f4-pq5hg.html) 
freeradius_nbi is OK - so onboarding/distrib/instantiation chain is OK
however several unexpected errors
- basic_vm => service instance creation (onboarding/distribution is OK)
- clearwater_ims => usual error due to // processing
- 5gbulkpm => this one should have been OK got empty message from message router - so not related to SO 
- pnf-registrate => usual hieratic error, no resources found on AAI

I noticed that the daily of the 8th is also not very good compared to the previous ones.
Ity is probably due to the expiration of aaf-cert as reported by Fiachra (not seen so far by my cert tests as I test the nodeports and not the internal node..but it is planned for G)

oom_redeploy to get some confirmations

Patch Set 1:

the commit message (on OOM) must follow this form:

[NAME_OF_COMPONENT|DOC|COMMON|GENERIC] Meaningful title (from OOM side)

at least one sentence explaining the change done in this patch, cause and consequences and possibly more of course

Issue-ID: AS_WE_ARE_FORCED_BUT_MEANINGLESS
Change-ID: xxx
Sign-off: xxx

Commit message will be the last stuff that will stay with our code so it must clearly explain the changes, the "why" and the consequences. If it change OOM behavior in any way, documentation must be also updated.

Starting Frankfurt branching, merge requests which are not following this pattern will not be merged.

Please read the following pages and follow the guidelines for writing commit message contained therein.

    http://bit.ly/goodcommitmessages
    http://who-t.blogspot.com/2009/12/on-commit-messages.html
    http://dep.debian.net/deps/dep3/
